events.title: US-Iran escalation threatens Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Oil prices have risen due to renewed US strikes in Iran and threats of further action. Iran has warned it may close the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil chokepoint, in response.

events.title: APAC coal demand surges due to Middle East conflict
events.description: The Middle East conflict has damaged Gulf energy infrastructure, prompting a 150 million tonne surge in thermal coal demand in the Asia-Pacific region as energy security becomes a priority.

events.title: Attack on tanker MT Settebello near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The International Maritime Organization condemned the attack on the tanker MT Settebello near the Strait of Hormuz, with three seafarers reported missing. The incident raises concerns over regional security and potential disruptions to maritime trade in a key global chokepoint.

events.title: ARA Hub Bunker Fuel Availability Tightens
events.description: Bunker fuel availability remains tight in the ARA hub, with recommended lead times for VLSFO and HSFO supplies now at 7-8 days. Independently held fuel oil stocks have remained flat in June.

events.title: Hormuz Strait normalisation delayed
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd CEO stated that normalisation of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will take at least three months after access is restored, indicating ongoing disruption to global freight flows.

events.title: Sanctioned Russian tankers bypassing English Channel
events.description: Western-sanctioned Russian oil tankers are rerouting to avoid the English Channel, with 84 tracked in the past 30 days. Many are flagged under third-party jurisdictions like Sierra Leone and Cameroon.

events.title: Japan refiners use ship transfers due to Hormuz disruptions
events.description: Japanese refiners are using VLCCs for ship-to-ship transfers in Malaysia and tapping the US market due to disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz, altering standard crude trade mechanisms.

events.title: Swire Shipping increases rates for Australian and Timor-Leste routes
events.description: Swire Shipping has announced General Rate Increases (GRIs) for cargo shipments to Townsville and Darwin in Australia, as well as Dili in Timor-Leste, effective June 25, 2026, due to rising operating costs and vessel charter rates.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd updates Africa-bound peak season surcharge
events.location: Asia and Oceania to West Africa and East Africa
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd has updated its Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) for cargo from Asia and Oceania to West Africa and East Africa, effective June 20, 2026. The surcharge adjustment reflects carrier pricing actions during peak shipping periods.

events.title: Kuwait airspace closure disrupts air cargo
events.description: Kuwait airspace was temporarily closed following strikes in the region, causing uncertainty for air cargo operations. DHL has restored its Gulf network, but airlines remain cautious about potential renewed disruptions.

events.title: EU imposes €3 fee on low-value e-commerce imports
events.description: The EU will implement a €3 fee for low-value e-commerce shipments starting 1 July, causing expected volatility in Asia-Europe air cargo markets. Importers are rushing to meet the deadline, with concerns about a surge in pre-deadline volumes and uncertain post-implementation demand patterns.

events.title: US considers revived China ship fees impacting ag exports
events.description: US agriculture exporters warn that revived port fees on Chinese-built ships could add up to $900 per container, threatening export sales of key commodities. Senators Elizabeth Warren and Mark Kelly have pushed for reinstating the fees.

events.title: Hormuz Strait closure impacts Middle East landbridges
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed following air strikes between Iran and the US, forcing Gulf importers to rely on overland routes and the TIR system. This has increased pressure on landbridges and caused container congestion at Saudi Red Sea ports like Jeddah and King Abdullah.

events.title: U.S. Forces Disable Tanker MT Jalveer in Gulf of Oman
events.description: U.S. forces disabled the MT Jalveer tanker during a blockade enforcement operation in the Gulf of Oman. All 20 Indian crew members were safely evacuated to shore.

events.title: Iran Closes Hormuz Strait to Vessel Traffic
events.description: Following US airstrikes, Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to all vessel traffic, raising concerns over global oil shipments and regional security.

events.title: US container imports decline in April
events.description: Imports through major U.S. container ports fell 5.1% in April compared to March and 7.3% compared to April 2025, according to the Global Port Tracker report.

events.title: EU sanctions drive new tanker sale clause
events.description: BIMCO is drafting a standard clause for tanker sales to comply with EU sanctions targeting Russian involvement in tanker trades, following the 20th sanctions package.

events.title: Three Gorges Dam ship lock expansion begins
events.description: China has started construction on a new five-stage ship lock system at the Three Gorges Dam, aiming to nearly double cargo capacity and reduce congestion on a major inland shipping corridor.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz ceasefire collapses
events.description: A fragile ceasefire in the Strait of Hormuz has collapsed following fresh attacks between the US and Iran. A product tanker, Settebello, suffered a fire off Oman, with three seafarers missing.

events.title: U.S. Policy Shift on Venezuela Energy and Mining Exports
events.description: The U.S. Treasury Department introduced a new sanctions framework to allow U.S. and allied companies to participate in Venezuela's oil, gas, and mineral exports, signaling a significant policy shift that could impact global supply chains.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz disruption raises freight rates
events.description: Freight rates are rising due to ongoing uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz, with bunker surcharges and expectations of prolonged disruption affecting market sentiment.

events.title: VLCC Disappearances Near Suez
events.description: Ships, particularly VLCCs, are disappearing under the radar, with notable activity reported on both sides of the Suez Canal, indicating potential disruptions in freight flows.

events.title: Ukraine targets Russian logistics in Mariupol
events.description: Ukraine is intensifying its military campaign, targeting Russian logistical operations in Mariupol and other areas, disrupting supply chain movements in the region.

events.title: Cargo ship engaged in gunfire with small boat off Yemen
events.description: A cargo ship exchanged gunfire with a small boat approximately 88 nautical miles from Balhaf, Yemen, in the Gulf of Aden, likely an attempted piracy incident.

events.title: Greek Ferry Damages Port Jetty
events.description: A car ferry collided with a jetty at the entrance to the port of Rafina, Greece, breaking it in half. The ferry sustained no damage, but the incident may affect port operations.

events.title: FedEx pilots ratify new labor agreement
events.description: FedEx pilots have ratified a new labor agreement with ALPA, covering approximately 5,000 pilots. The contract will take effect on June 29 and marks a significant development in the company's airline operations.

events.title: U.S. Operation Assists Ships Through Hormuz Strait
events.description: President Trump claimed the U.S. military conducted a covert operation to assist over 200 commercial ships through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, indicating potential geopolitical tensions or disruptions in this key shipping route.

events.title: Truck-to-ship LNG bunkering in Las Palmas
events.description: A dual-fuel ferry was supplied with LNG via truck-to-ship bunkering at the Port of Las Palmas, marking a first for the port and supporting cleaner maritime operations.

events.title: Middle East emergency transit ports activated
events.description: Shipments to Upper Gulf locations are being rerouted and temporarily stored at emergency transit ports due to ongoing disruptions in Middle East trade corridors.

events.title: M/T MARIVEX registration clarification by Palau Ship Registry
events.description: The Palau Ship Registry clarifies that the vessel M/T MARIVEX (IMO 9464156) was not registered under the Palau flag at the time of an incident in the Gulf of Oman, addressing potential confusion in media reports.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Tensions Disrupt Shipping
events.description: Increased tensions between the U.S. and Iran are causing uncertainty about shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil transit route.

events.title: Product tanker attacked off Oman, crew missing
events.description: A Palau-flagged product tanker was attacked early on June 10, approximately 20 nautical miles off Sohar, Oman, leaving three crew members missing. The vessel issued a distress call following the incident.

events.title: Suez Canal implements new surcharge regime
events.description: Egypt has introduced a new surcharge regime for the Suez Canal, marking a shift toward ongoing toll extraction for strategic waterways. This affects shipping costs for vessels returning to traditional routes.

events.title: Organized cargo theft targeting Arizona rail corridor
events.description: Arizona investigators report that organized cargo theft crews are frequently targeting rail corridors in Northern Arizona, with train burglaries occurring up to a dozen times each month.

events.title: Democrats push to reinstate China port tax
events.description: Leading Democratic senators are advocating for the U.S. to reinstate a tax on Chinese ships, following a mutual postponement agreement between Trump and Xi through November.

events.title: Suez Canal sees oil tanker surge due to Hormuz closure
events.description: Oil tanker traffic through the Suez Canal increased by nearly a third in April as the Strait of Hormuz remained closed, prompting a shift to an alternative Red Sea energy route.

events.title: US Fuel Shipment to Cuba Post-Cold War
events.description: A Florida trading company is in advanced talks to send Cuba the largest U.S. fuel shipment since the Cold War, amid the island's severe energy crisis.

events.title: U.S. forces disable Iranian tanker in Gulf of Oman
events.description: At least two crew members were reported missing and one injured after U.S. forces disabled an Iranian tanker in the Gulf of Oman, marking the first reported seafarer casualties in the ongoing enforcement campaign.

events.title: Suspected Pirate Attack on Vessel Off Yemen
events.description: A merchant vessel transiting off Yemen's southern coast exchanged gunfire with an armed small craft, in an incident suspected to be a pirate attack. The vessel's armed security team repelled the threat.

events.title: Oil tankers cluster off Oman to bypass Hormuz chokepoint
events.description: Sixteen oil tankers clustered off the coast of Oman to transfer stranded Gulf oil barrels, likely to circumvent disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz. This activity indicates a significant shift in shipping tactics due to ongoing regional tensions and chokepoint constraints.

events.title: CMA CGM VENDOME completes Suez Canal transit
events.description: The mega container ship CMA CGM VENDOME completed its first transit through the Suez Canal on June 9, sailing northbound from France to Malaysia. The vessel is 399.9 meters long and 61.3 meters wide, with a 16-meter draft.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd adds PSS for Australia routes
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd has announced a Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) for container shipments from the Far East, Indian Subcontinent, Red Sea, and Middle East to Australia, effective July 15, 2026.

events.title: Brazil delays biofouling rule enforcement
events.description: Brazil has delayed full enforcement of its biofouling regulations by 18 months, but shipowners could still face fines of up to BRL50m ($9m) for non-compliance when the rules eventually take effect.

events.title: Suspected US strike on tanker off Oman injures, crew missing
events.description: Two crew members were missing and one injured after a suspected U.S. missile strike on a tanker off the coast of Oman. Maritime officials confirmed the incident on Wednesday.

events.title: Suez Canal sees oil tanker surge due to Hormuz closure
events.description: Oil tanker traffic through the Suez Canal increased by nearly a third in April as vessels diverted from the closed Strait of Hormuz, boosting canal revenue to its highest since early 2024.

events.title: Iraq and UAE expand oil pipelines amid Hormuz closure
events.description: Iraq and the UAE are accelerating plans to expand oil pipelines to offset lost capacity from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Iraq is fast-tracking crude exports via the Kurdistan-Turkey pipeline network.

events.title: Iranian exports collapse under enforcement pressure
events.description: Iranian crude and condensate exports dropped 84% in May to 186,000 barrels per day amid intensified enforcement actions against sanctioned vessels and heightened maritime control activity in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz disruption boosts US crude exports
events.description: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz reduced Middle Eastern crude availability, prompting a surge in US crude oil exports to 5.6 million b/d in May as global buyers sought alternative sources.

events.title: EU Prepares Port Investment Controls to Counter China
events.description: EU transport ministers are drafting guidance to assess foreign investment in ports and counter China's growing influence in the bloc's maritime infrastructure.

events.title: China-North America freight rates surge over 50%
events.description: Freight rates on China-North America shipping routes have increased over 50% since late April due to tight shipping capacity and strong demand for US-bound imports. Most space is already booked through June, signaling ongoing supply chain constraints.

events.title: Europe container markets tightening as carriers restrict capacity
events.description: Europe container markets are showing signs of tightening with rising spot rates and reduced carrier capacity, according to Sogese's latest report. Despite a traditionally slower season, carriers are implementing capacity controls and longer routing structures, reshaping freight market conditions.

events.title: Container rate surge due to peak season and geopolitical tensions
events.description: Container freight rates from East Asia and China to the US are surging due to peak season demand and the ongoing Iran-US conflict. Carriers are capitalizing on the high demand, with rate increases expected to continue through the end of July.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz remains highly volatile
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz continues to experience high volatility with ongoing security risks, including reports of seafarers being killed, injured, or detained. The IMO warns of the dangers of transiting the strait without credible security measures.

events.title: Chinese firm challenges Darwin port sale
events.description: A Chinese conglomerate has filed for arbitration over the forced sale of Darwin Port in Australia, signaling a strategic move amid broader geopolitical tensions.

events.title: IMO warns of seafarer risks in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: IMO Secretary-General Arsenio Dominguez has warned against sending vessels through the Strait of Hormuz without credible security guarantees due to escalating risks. The warning highlights growing concerns over seafarer safety in the region.

events.title: JNPA ground rent relief due to truck shortages
events.description: Nhava Sheva Port (JNPA) has provided 50% ground rent relief for import containers delayed due to truck shortages and yard congestion. The relief applies to containers exceeding free storage limits, easing some financial pressure on shippers.

events.title: Cargo ship spills petroleum off Luzon
events.description: A bow-ramp deck cargo ship ran aground off the northwest coast of Luzon on June 8, partially capsizing and spilling petroleum into the water.

events.title: Savannah Port opens chilled cargo inspection facility
events.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection will open a refrigerated inspection facility at the Port of Savannah on July 1, 2026, to streamline inspections of temperature-sensitive imports and reduce cold chain disruptions.

events.title: Suspension of Section 301 port fees on Chinese vessels
events.description: The Trump administration suspended Section 301 port fees on Chinese-linked vessels in November 2025 as part of a trade settlement with China. The suspension is now running out of road, signaling potential policy shifts that could impact port operations and freight flows.

events.title: CNC upgrades JTVS service for faster Japan-Asia links
events.description: CNC has enhanced its Japan Thailand Vietnam Service (JTVS) with a streamlined port rotation to improve schedule reliability and intra-Asia connectivity, featuring weekly connections across key ports in Japan, South Korea, Vietnam, and Thailand.

events.title: Russian shadow fleet guards on sanctioned oil tankers
events.description: Dozens of Russian men with military and security backgrounds are deployed aboard tankers carrying sanctioned Russian oil, according to an investigation by OCCRP and European media partners. This indicates a covert industry effort to circumvent sanctions and maintain oil exports.

events.title: Bunker Fuel Supply Constraints in Singapore
events.description: Very Low Sulfur Fuel Oil (VLSFO) supply in Singapore remains constrained with lead times of 8–14 days. Heavy Fuel Oil (HSFO) availability has improved slightly, but LSMGO supply has tightened.

events.title: El Nino poses risk to Asian coal and food trade
events.description: The World Economic Forum warns that an emerging El Nino weather pattern could disrupt Asian coal demand and agricultural trade flows through mid-2026, posing a systemic risk to global markets.

events.title: MSC Elsa 3 Containership Sinks Off India Coast
events.description: A year after the MSC Elsa 3 containership sank off the coast of India, the Directorate General of Shipping reported that maintenance and training issues were likely causes of the incident, which disrupted freight operations.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Closure Impact on Oil Supply
events.description: Markets anticipate an oil shortage if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, with potential oversupply once it reopens. The situation highlights significant uncertainty in global energy supply chains.

events.title: Russian paramilitary presence on tankers near Oresund
events.description: Danish pilots have reported the presence of uniformed Russian paramilitary personnel aboard Russia-linked tankers passing through the Oresund Strait, raising concerns about potential security risks and geopolitical tensions affecting maritime traffic.

events.title: Suez Canal Increases Transit Surcharges
events.description: The Suez Canal Authority has announced its first significant rate increases in three years, implementing revised surcharges for transits. The move reflects ongoing efforts to boost transit volumes and revenue.

events.title: Maersk exceeds self-imposed capacity cap
events.description: Maersk Line is set to exceed its 4.3 million TEU capacity cap by chartering additional vessels, pushing its capacity to 4.7 million TEU due to firming demand and rising freight rates.

events.title: U.S. Army helicopter shot down in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A U.S. Army helicopter was shot down by Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz, leading to a rescue operation. The incident raises concerns over regional security and potential disruptions to maritime traffic in a key global chokepoint.

events.title: Cargo ship helmsman arrested after collision
events.description: A helmsman of a general cargo ship was arrested in Copenhagen after the vessel he was operating collided with a sailboat and a pier. The incident occurred under suspicion of drunk driving.

events.title: EU Cracks Down on Mediterranean Dark Fleet
events.description: The European Union has initiated coordinated stop-and-search operations in the Mediterranean to target dark fleet vessels involved in fraud, false flagging, or regulatory breaches, potentially disrupting maritime trade flows.

events.title: Dutch intercept goods violating Russian sanctions
events.description: Dutch Customs has arrested a captain and intercepted containers violating Russian sanctions. The enforcement action is part of a broader crackdown at the Port of Rotterdam to prevent sanctioned goods from reaching Russia.

events.title: U.S. tariffs trigger early trans-Pacific shipping surge
events.description: New U.S. tariffs are prompting importers to frontload cargo, causing an early surge in trans-Pacific freight volumes and rising shipping rates. This disrupts the expected shift to a late peak season and strains port and logistics capacity.

events.title: Hormuz Disruption Impact on Oil Demand
events.description: The EIA has noted that weak global oil demand could limit price spikes caused by ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting the chokepoint's continued impact on energy supply chains.

events.title: Suez Canal Surcharges Increase
events.description: Suez Canal surcharges will increase starting July 15, 2026, affecting vessels transiting the canal. This policy change may impact shipping costs and logistics planning.

events.title: EU Proposes Sanctions on Russian Bunker and Support Vessels
events.description: The European Commission has proposed sanctions targeting Russian bunker and support vessels as part of the 21st EU sanctions package against Russia. The move aims to intensify Western pressure on Russia and disrupt its maritime logistics and energy supply chains.

events.title: Panama Canal reduces Neopanamax draft limit
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority will lower the maximum draft for Neopanamax vessels to 49.5 feet starting July 1, a precautionary measure due to water shortage concerns.

events.title: FedEx pilots approve new contract after labor disputes
events.description: FedEx pilots have approved a new collective bargaining agreement, resolving years of labor unrest. The resolution is expected to stabilize operations and prevent potential disruptions in air freight services.

events.title: Containership orderbook hits 39% of fleet
events.description: The containership orderbook to fleet ratio has reached 39%, the highest since 2010, with 1,630 ships ordered for 13.28m teu. This indicates strong industry confidence and potential future capacity increases.

events.title: EU Imposes Sanctions on Russia's Shadow Fleet and Energy Exports
events.description: The European Commission proposed its 21st sanctions package against Russia, targeting financial institutions, shadow fleet operations, LNG tankers, and military supply chains, disrupting trade and logistics flows.

events.title: Ship traffic through Strait of Hormuz rises
events.description: U.S. Energy Secretary reports a meaningful increase in ship traffic and oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions between Washington and Tehran.

events.title: IMO Warns of Unsafe Conditions in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) chief has warned that the Strait of Hormuz remains too dangerous for safe passage, despite increased traffic claims. The warning highlights ongoing risks to global shipping through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure disrupts LNG trade
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz and damage to Qatar’s LNG infrastructure have caused a 24% monthly increase in the World Bank Group’s natural gas price index, disrupting global LNG trade.

events.title: Fragile Israel-Iran truce, Hormuz blockade ongoing
events.description: A fragile ceasefire between Israel and Iran has led to a drop in oil prices, but the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz continues, posing a risk to global oil shipments.

events.title: Indonesia tightens coal export curbs
events.description: Indonesia has tightened export controls on thermal coal, causing prices to rise above $150 per ton and delaying shipments. The move is linked to seasonal demand increases and ongoing regional disruptions.

events.title: MSC revives Pearl transpacific service for early peak season
events.description: MSC has reintroduced its Pearl transpacific service to address early peak season demand, with a 42-day rotation including Yantian, Xiamen, and Long Beach.

events.title: Kuehne+Nagel adds Frankfurt to air freight route
events.description: Kuehne+Nagel has expanded its air freight network by adding Frankfurt to its B747-8 freighter rotation between North America, Europe, and Asia, enhancing connectivity and focusing on growing European air cargo demand.

events.title: Gulf crisis driving freight cost surge
events.description: Rising freight costs are being driven by the ongoing Gulf crisis, with the Global Price Index increasing by over 12% in April due to heightened tensions and early peak season demand.

events.title: Kuwait resumes oil exports to Asia
events.description: Kuwait is offering to sell crude oil to Asian refiners for the first time since the Iran war began, signaling increased oil flows through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Ships Break Away During Storm Due to Delayed Emergency Assistance
events.description: Delayed emergency assistance contributed to three containerships and a vehicle carrier breaking away during a storm. The incident highlights risks to maritime safety and potential disruptions to freight flows.

events.title: Container vessel sinks off Batam
events.description: The Tanzania-registered container vessel GOLDEN STAR 1 sank approximately 6 km off Batam, Indonesia, on June 5. All nine crew members were rescued safely with no reported injuries.

events.title: MSC updates Emergency Fuel Surcharge for European short-sea trades
events.description: Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) has announced an updated Emergency Fuel Surcharge (EFS) for cargo moving between Northern Europe, the North East Mediterranean, intra-Europe short-sea markets, and intra-Greece, Türkiye, and Black Sea trades, effective 1 July 2026.

events.title: Kuehne+Nagel expands air freight network with Frankfurt hub
events.description: Kuehne+Nagel has added Frankfurt to its Inspire aircraft rotation, enhancing transatlantic pharmaceutical connectivity and strengthening its own-controlled air freight network across North America, Europe, and Asia.

events.title: Russia reopens Arctic LNG route to Asia
events.description: Russia has reactivated its Arctic LNG export route to Asia as the icebreaking LNG carrier Christophe de Margerie completed a rare early-summer transit along the Northern Sea Route, marking the start of the 2026 navigation season.

events.title: ADNOC increases oil exports amid Hormuz Strait tensions
events.description: Despite an Iranian blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, the UAE's ADNOC is increasing its crude oil export sales, indicating continued activity despite regional tensions affecting a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Kuehne+Nagel expands Inspire air freight network
events.description: Kuehne+Nagel has added Frankfurt to its Inspire air freight network, enhancing connections between North America, Europe, and Asia. The expansion supports pharmaceutical and time-sensitive cargo shipments.

events.title: CMA CGM implements new Asia-Africa PSS
events.description: CMA CGM introduced a new Peak Season Surcharge (PSS) of $500 per TEU for shipments from Southeast Asia to West Africa, effective June 15, 2026. The surcharge reflects industry signals related to freight pricing adjustments.

events.title: Van Hui Shipping acquires capesize vessel Ehime Queen
events.description: Van Hui Shipping has acquired the 181,000 dwt capesize vessel Ehime Queen, previously owned by Nissen Kaiun. The vessel, built in Imabari, Japan, was sold for $57.5 million, indicating strong market interest in capesize ships.

events.title: Suez Canal raises transit charges mid-July
events.description: The Suez Canal Authority will increase transit surcharges for most vessel types starting July 15, adding to standard dues and potentially impacting shipping costs and routing decisions.

events.title: Indian Seafarers Rescued from Abandoned Ship
events.description: Four Indian seafarers were rescued after being stranded on a derelict cargo ship for 10 months. The Turkish government intervened to secure their release and ensure their safety.

events.title: X-Press Feeders launches China-East Mediterranean service
events.description: X-Press Feeders has announced a new direct China-East Mediterranean X-PRESS (CEX) service, starting June 23, 2026, enhancing freight connectivity between China, the Red Sea, and the East Mediterranean.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz disruption impacts oil tanker demand
events.description: Maritime disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz are altering global oil trade flows, potentially threatening an extended decline in tanker demand despite alternative routes adding to ton-mile requirements.

events.title: EU Sanctions Iran Over Strait of Hormuz Navigation
events.description: The European Council imposed sanctions, including on Iran’s IRGC, while calling for freedom of navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: U.S. Senators Push to Reimpose Port Fees on Chinese Ships
events.description: U.S. Senators Mark Kelly and Elizabeth Warren are urging the Trump administration to reinstate port fees on Chinese-linked vessels, which were previously suspended. The senators argue the fees are essential for rebuilding U.S. infrastructure and addressing trade imbalances.

events.title: Norwegian Customs to Implement Digitoll Maritime Reporting
events.description: Starting September 15, 2026, Norwegian Customs will enforce strict new digital reporting requirements called 'Digitoll' for all maritime traffic, affecting supply chain operations.

events.title: Houthis ban Israeli shipping in Red Sea
events.description: Yemen's Houthis declared a complete ban on Israeli maritime navigation in the Red Sea, following a missile attack on Israel. The move raises concerns about renewed disruption to this critical global shipping route.

events.title: Indian crew evacuated from burning tanker off Oman
events.description: An Indian crew was airlifted from a burning shadow fleet tanker disabled by U.S. forces off the coast of Oman. The incident occurred after a fire broke out aboard the vessel, forcing the crew to abandon ship.

events.title: Revived Houthi Blockade Disrupts Shipping
events.description: A revived Houthi blockade in the Red Sea is contributing to a shipping crisis, alongside dark transits and secondary rate contagion, creating significant disruptions to global freight flows.

events.title: Alleged carrier impersonation scheme causes $4.49M in cargo losses
events.description: Eight individuals have been indicted for allegedly impersonating trucking carriers and diverting millions in freight, which was then allegedly moved, stored, and resold. The scheme involved six separate theft incidents across multiple states.

events.title: US railroads mandated to report weekly performance data
events.description: Starting July 8, US Class I railroads must submit weekly performance metrics to the Surface Transportation Board (STB), aiming to improve accountability and transparency in rail operations.

events.title: Houthis Threaten Red Sea Shipping, Impact Oil Market
events.description: Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis announced a ban on ships linked to Israel from the Red Sea, escalating tensions and raising concerns over global shipping and energy flows.

events.title: U.S. Disables Sanctioned Tanker in Gulf of Oman
events.description: U.S. forces disabled a sanctioned shadow-fleet tanker bound for Iran in the Gulf of Oman, the seventh such interdiction since the imposition of a maritime blockade. The action reflects ongoing enforcement of trade restrictions affecting global shipping flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure impacts LNG supply
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has removed 20% of global LNG supply, equivalent to 80 Mtpa, leading to significant market uncertainty and potential long-term price increases.

events.title: Israel strikes Iranian petrochemical plant
events.description: Israel struck a petrochemical plant and other military targets in southwestern Iran, escalating regional tensions and threatening a fragile ceasefire. The attack has raised concerns over potential disruptions to energy supply chains and regional freight flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Disrupts Oil and LNG Flows
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz has been closed to commercial shipping since Feb. 28, disrupting 25% of global seaborne oil and 20% of LNG trade. Traffic has dropped to a trickle, significantly impacting global energy supply chains.

events.title: Middle East conflict drives freight rate spikes
events.description: Freight rates are surging due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East and growing fears of an energy crisis, according to Xeneta's latest market update.

events.title: Fuel shortages could idle 10% of global shipping fleet
events.description: Mercuria's shipping head warns that fuel shortages, exacerbated by the Middle East conflict, could ground up to 10% of the global fleet. The crisis is linked to disruptions in diesel and jet fuel exports from the region.

events.title: Israeli counterattacks in Iran raise Red Sea transit risks
events.description: Israel has launched counterattacks across Iran following missile strikes from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). These actions are raising concerns about potential disruptions to Red Sea shipping routes.

events.title: Intra-Asia container freight rates hit record high
events.description: The Drewry Intra-Asia Container Index reached a record high of $1,114 per 40-foot container, driven by strong demand and market uncertainty following recent geopolitical tensions.

events.title: APM Terminals reduces rail capacity at Rotterdam's Maasvlakte II
events.description: APM Terminals is temporarily reducing rail handling capacity at its Maasvlakte II terminal in Rotterdam by approximately 40% from July 2026 due to expansion and testing works, affecting rail operations between the terminal yard and rail area.

events.title: CMA CGM imposes fuel surcharges on India-Africa cargo
events.description: CMA CGM has introduced significant fuel surcharges for cargo moving between India and African countries including South Africa, Tanzania, Mozambique, and Kenya due to rising fuel costs.

events.title: Iran war impacts Hormuz Strait trade
events.description: The ongoing US-Iran conflict has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, with the OECD revising global GDP growth downward due to a 'time-limited disruption' scenario. Despite economic concerns, container volumes remain strong.

events.title: Transpacific spot rates surge to near-record levels
events.description: Container spot rates on the transpacific trade routes have surged to near-record levels, according to Sea-Intelligence. The sharp increase raises concerns about potential instability in the market, with the World Container Index (WCI) showing exceptionally strong week-on-week gains.

events.title: Gemini carriers shift capacity to Asia-Mediterranean route
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd and Maersk, via the Gemini Cooperation, are shifting capacity from other major trade lanes to the Asia-Mediterranean route, signaling a strategic market share push in this trade corridor.

events.title: Panama Canal reduces vessel draft limits
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority has reduced the maximum draft for vessels using neopanamax locks, raising concerns about potential disruptions similar to those seen during the previous drought crisis. The new limits, effective July 3, could impact global shipping operations.

events.title: Middle East conflict escalates, threatening shipping routes
events.description: Middle East tensions have intensified following Israeli strikes on Iran, raising concerns for global shipping markets already disrupted by the Strait of Hormuz and Red Sea threats. The conflict could further impact critical maritime freight flows.

events.title: Houthis ban Israeli ships in Red Sea
events.description: The Houthis, an Iran-backed group in Yemen, have declared a complete ban on Israeli ships in the Red Sea, potentially disrupting a key shipping route near the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: EU Sanctions IRGC Navy Unit Over Hormuz Strait Navigation
events.description: The European Union has imposed sanctions on an Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy unit and two individuals over alleged attempts to restrict navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Tanker Fire Near Oman’s Masirah Island
events.description: A tanker caught fire off the coast of Oman near Masirah Island, leading to the evacuation of its crew and a joint response from Omani and Indian authorities.

events.title: Drone Explosion in Constanta Port
events.description: A Ukrainian naval drone was found and later detonated in the Port of Constanta, Romania, raising concerns about security and potential disruptions to port operations.

events.title: Red Sea shipping disrupted by Houthi and regional conflict escalation
events.description: Ongoing conflict between Israel, Iran, and Houthi rebels has intensified, leading to heavy strikes and regional instability. The situation has disrupted Red Sea shipping, a critical global freight route.

events.title: Iran demands $2M for Hormuz Strait transit
events.description: Iran has been accused of extortion for demanding up to $2 million from vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about global energy security and potential disruptions to shipping.

events.title: Middle East conflict causes freight rate surges
events.description: Containerized imports and exports in the Middle East dropped sharply in March following conflict escalation, with imports down 64% and exports down 62% year-on-year, signaling major disruption to regional freight flows.

events.title: Sailors trapped by Strait of Hormuz blockade
events.description: Sailors report stress and exhaustion after being trapped for months due to a blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Panama Canal reduces Neopanamax draft limit
events.description: The Panama Canal has lowered the maximum authorized draft for Neopanamax vessels to 49.5 feet (15.09 meters), effective July 1, due to low Gatun Lake water levels. This adjustment impacts vessel capacity and transit efficiency through the canal.

events.title: US Section 301 tariffs drive shipping surcharges
events.description: Container shipping carriers are imposing peak season surcharges due to increased demand as US importers rush to avoid upcoming Section 301 tariffs. The USTR proposed new tariffs on Canada and the EU, further impacting freight flows.

events.title: Hormuz shipping crisis impacts tanker market
events.description: The ongoing Hormuz shipping crisis is influencing tanker rates as operators assess how shadow-fleet scrapping and newbuild order books will affect supply and demand dynamics.

events.title: CMA CGM orders 8 new 6,000 TEU boxships at Hengli
events.description: French shipping giant CMA CGM has placed an order for eight new 6,000 TEU container ships at Hengli Heavy Industries in China, expanding its already large orderbook and signaling continued investment in fleet capacity.

events.title: Taiwan Coast Guard expels Chinese ships near southern waters
events.description: Taiwan's coast guard reported expelling four Chinese government ships that entered restricted waters off the island's southern coast, escalating tensions in the region.

events.title: Majestic X Tanker Seizure Linked to Kinahan Cartel and IRGC
events.description: The seizure of the OFAC-sanctioned VLCC tanker Majestic X (IMO 9198317) has been linked to Iran's IRGC and the Kinahan drugs cartel, raising concerns about illicit activities impacting maritime security and supply chain flows.

events.title: U.S. Sanctions 6 Iran-Linked LPG Carriers
events.description: The U.S. Treasury has blacklisted six more LPG carriers linked to Iran for transporting Iranian gas, increasing pressure on Iran's shipping activities and potentially disrupting global LPG trade routes.

events.title: Container Feeder Sinks off Singapore
events.description: A small container ship sank in the Strait of Singapore shortly after departing the pier on Friday night. All crew members were rescued.

events.title: MSC discontinues Indus Express service
events.description: MSC is discontinuing its Indus Express service, which connected the US East Coast with the Middle East and Indian Subcontinent. The service, operational since 2014, has been subject to recent network adjustments.

events.title: CMA CGM increases FAK rates from Indian Subcontinent
events.description: CMA CGM announced increased Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates from the Indian Subcontinent to Latin America, Europe, and North Africa, effective June 10, 2026, indicating ongoing pricing pressures in global trade lanes.

events.title: TurkStream gas halted for maintenance
events.description: Russian gas deliveries to Europe via the TurkStream pipeline have been halted due to planned maintenance at the Russkaya pumping station and receiving terminals in Turkey, starting June 2 and expected to continue through June.

events.title: US shoots down Iran drones near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The US military shot down two Iranian one-way attack drones near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, raising tensions in the region and threatening international maritime traffic.

events.title: Far East–India shipping service restructured
events.description: The joint Far East–India service operated by Emirates, Evergreen, Gold Star, and KMTC is being restructured into two separate service arrangements, potentially affecting freight flows between the regions.

events.title: Drewry WCI rises 23% due to early peak season demand
events.description: Container freight rates surged 23% week-on-week, with the Drewry World Container Index reaching $3,433 per 40-foot container, driven by strong demand in Transpacific and Asia-Europe trade routes.

events.title: China–South Africa shipping network restructured
events.description: The China–South Africa container shipping network is undergoing significant changes as Evergreen and PIL exit, impacting the service structure previously involving five carriers. This shift may affect freight flows and reliability on the route.

events.title: Russian forces attack Ukrainian rescue vessels
events.description: Russian forces attacked two civilian search and rescue vessels in Ukrainian waters, causing injuries. The incident was reported by Ukrainian Deputy Prime Minister Oleksiy Kuleba.

events.title: Chinese ships near Taiwan in South China Sea
events.description: A Chinese coast guard ship and a survey ship conducted a coordinated operation near Taiwan's key islands, escalating tensions in the South China Sea.

events.title: Asia-US container rates surge due to Iran war
events.description: Container shipping rates from Asia to the US have spiked 109% since the Iran war began, driven by rising fuel costs, port congestion, and geopolitical tensions affecting key maritime routes.

events.title: US-IRGC clashes in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: US forces have launched counterattacks in response to several IRGC attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over regional security and potential disruptions to maritime traffic.

events.title: US Tightens Controls on Iranian Dark Fleet
events.description: On June 2, the US Treasury's OFAC imposed new sanctions targeting Iranian dark-fleet operations, aiming to disrupt illicit shipping activities and tighten control over Iranian vessels involved in sanctions evasion.

events.title: Containership sinks off Batam
events.description: The Tanzania-flagged containership Golden Star 1 has sunk off the Indonesian island of Batam after taking on water, prompting navigation warnings in a major shipping corridor.

events.title: Osaka Port container volumes decline 0.5% in April
events.description: The Port of Osaka reported a 0.5% year-on-year decline in container volumes in April, with exports down 2.9% to 83,371 TEUs, while imports rose 1.7% to 97,229 TEUs.

events.title: MSC restructures Wallaby service, separates New Zealand routes
events.description: MSC has restructured its Wallaby service, removing New Zealand port calls and shifting them to a separate feeder connection to focus on Australia-bound cargo flows.

events.title: Brazil-Europe Green Shipping Corridor Initiative
events.location: Port of Açu and Port of Antwerp-Bruges
events.description: A consortium led by the Global Maritime Forum and RMI is working to establish a green shipping corridor between Brazil's Port of Açu and Belgium's Port of Antwerp-Bruges. The initiative involves assessing infrastructure, vessels, and business models to support sustainable shipping practices.

events.title: K LINE orders LNG-fuelled car carriers for Europe
events.description: Kawasaki Kisen Kaisha (K LINE) has ordered four LNG dual-fuel car carriers to enhance its European short-sea shipping operations. The vessels will be built by China Merchants Jinling Shipyard and operated by K LINE’s European subsidiary, KESS.

events.title: Hormuz conflict impacts shipping fuel strategy
events.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is disrupting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, leading to increased operational costs and a shift in the viability of alternative fuels in the shipping industry.

events.title: Indonesian coal exports decline in 2026
events.description: Seaborne coal exports from Indonesia have declined in the first four months of 2026, according to Banchero Costa. The drop in exports is based on vessel tracking data and may indicate a shift in global coal demand or supply dynamics.

events.title: Ukrainian drone explodes in Romanian port
events.description: A Ukrainian sea drone exploded in Constanta Harbor, Romania. No injuries were reported, but the incident is under investigation.

events.title: Panama Canal reduces draft limits due to El Niño
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority announced a reduction in the maximum authorized draft for Neopanamax vessels due to the El Niño weather forecast, which could impact transit efficiency and cargo capacity.

events.title: Ukrainian attacks on cargo ships kill Azerbaijani seafarers
events.description: Five Azerbaijani seafarers were killed and several injured in Ukrainian attacks on commercial ships in the Black Sea region, disrupting freight flows and raising safety concerns for maritime operations.

events.title: U.S. reports higher Hormuz Strait vessel transits
events.description: U.S. Central Command reports nearly 1,000 commercial vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz in two months, exceeding private tracking data that relies on ship transponders.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz shutdown raises oil market uncertainty
events.description: Oil market experts are divided on the impact of a potential prolonged shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil transit chokepoint, as U.S. oil inventories fall to a 22-year low.

events.title: US boards sanctioned stateless tanker with Iranian oil
events.description: The United States has boarded a sanctioned stateless tanker in the Indian Ocean, reportedly carrying Iranian oil. The action was taken by U.S. Indo-Pacific Command as part of ongoing efforts to interdict vessels linked to Iran.

events.title: Omani Oil Exports Resume After Drone Attack
events.description: Oman's oil export terminal at Mina Al Fahal resumed normal operations on June 5 following a drone attack that had temporarily disrupted exports.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd discontinues CCM service to Mexico/Central America
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd announced it will discontinue its CCM service connecting Mexico and Central America, with the final sailing scheduled for July 2026. The service currently calls at Lázaro Cárdenas, Acajutla, and Corinto.

events.title: Panama Canal Neopanamax draft limit reduction
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority will reduce the maximum draft for Neopanamax vessels starting July 3 due to concerns over potential El Niño conditions, which could impact transit capacity and shipping efficiency.

events.title: Mercuria uses government deals to move oil through Hormuz
events.description: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. has successfully moved oil through the Strait of Hormuz via government-brokered deals, according to legal filings, highlighting how traders are managing the critical chokepoint amid the Iran war.

events.title: U.S. Boards Iranian Sanctioned Tanker in Indian Ocean
events.description: U.S. forces boarded a sanctioned Iranian tanker in the Indian Ocean as part of an expanded maritime enforcement campaign targeting Iran's oil trade. The action reflects heightened tensions and potential disruptions to regional shipping routes.

events.title: Hormuz Crisis Disrupts Container Shipping
events.description: Container freight rates are surging due to the conflict in the Middle East and disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. Rising tensions and energy crisis fears are compounding the situation during peak shipping season.

events.title: Iran charges toll for Hormuz Strait transit
events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps is charging $2 million per voyage for transit authorization through the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting standard shipping procedures and raising concerns over increased costs and potential geopolitical tensions.

events.title: Hormuz transit payments impact diplomatic tensions
events.description: Below-threshold transit payments are affecting diplomatic relations and freight flows through the strategic Strait of Hormuz before any formal agreement is reached.

events.title: India-Gulf container rates drop due to eased backlogs
events.description: Container shipping rates from India to the Persian Gulf have dropped by up to 40% as capacity returns and cargo backlogs ease. Current rates are around $2,100 per 20ft and $3,200 per 40ft container.

events.title: Container spot rates surge due to peak season and tight capacity
events.description: Spot rates for container shipping on transpacific and Asia-Europe routes have risen sharply amid peak season demand and constrained capacity. Carriers have implemented higher FAK rates and new peak season surcharges for contracted shippers.

events.title: US forces board sanctioned tanker Davina in Indian Ocean
events.description: U.S. forces conducted an interdiction of the sanctioned stateless oil tanker Davina in the Indian Ocean, according to the U.S. military's Indo-Pacific Command.

events.title: Singapore secures LNG supply to offset Qatar disruption
events.description: Singapore has secured replacement LNG cargoes to offset disrupted supplies from Qatar through 2026, according to the Energy Market Authority. Qatar previously supplied about 10% of Singapore's LNG needs before the Middle East conflict.

events.title: Fuel availability tight in Houston
events.description: Heavy congestion and delays at Houston fuel loading terminals are causing tight availability for bunker fuel, with lead times of 8-10 days for HSFO and VLSFO.

events.title: Containership transits through Suez Canal slow
events.description: Containership transits through the Suez Canal have slowed, with 20 vessels passing in the week ended 24 May and 26 in the following week, according to the Drewry Red Sea Diversion Tracker. This indicates a continued but reduced impact of the Red Sea diversion trend on global freight flows.

events.title: MSC reinstates Transpacific Pearl service
events.description: MSC is reintroducing its Transpacific Pearl service on the Asia–US trade lane due to improved market demand, following its withdrawal in mid-2025.

events.title: Sanctioned Russian LNG carrier transits Arctic route
events.description: The sanctioned Russian LNG carrier Christophe de Margerie made a rare early-season eastbound transit along the Northern Sea Route, escorted by the nuclear icebreaker Ural. This marks only the second time such a voyage has occurred at this time of year, highlighting potential shifts in Arctic shipping activity.

events.title: Middle East conflict impacts bunker fuel market
events.description: The global bunker market remains volatile due to ongoing uncertainty from the Middle East conflict, affecting fuel prices and trade stability.

events.title: Container spot rates surge due to Red Sea disruptions and peak season
events.description: Container shipping spot rates have surged to their highest in years due to Red Sea disruptions, early peak season demand, and aggressive carrier pricing. The Shanghai Containerized Freight Index (SCFI) rose 155 points to 2,726.48.

events.title: European mine-clearance force forms for Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A European mine-clearance force is being assembled for the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions between Iran and the United States. The move signals potential risks to maritime traffic in this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: GEODIS opens new port hub in Le Havre
events.description: GEODIS has launched its first integrated port hub in Le Havre, France, consolidating its four business lines into a single logistics platform at the country's largest container port to enhance import and export operations.

events.title: Panama Canal congestion at yearly high
events.description: Panama Canal congestion has reached its highest level this year, leading to Jones Act waivers for domestic shipments. The Panama Canal Authority plans to maintain full transit capacity through December despite maintenance and El Niño risks.

events.title: Hormuz Strait traffic in potential limbo post-conflict
events.description: Experts suggest that even after the conflict in Iran ends, control of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz may remain in a gray zone, neither fully closed nor fully open, potentially disrupting long-term freight flows.

events.title: Cargo ship seized in Sweden over Ukraine grain theft
events.description: A Swedish court approved the seizure of a cargo ship accused of stealing grain from occupied regions of Ukraine. The vessel is being held pending legal action related to alleged theft and smuggling activities.

events.title: Ukraine Sea Corridor Exports Top 200 Million Tons
events.description: Ukraine reports that over 200 million tons of exports have been shipped via the Black Sea corridor since its establishment in September 2023, indicating improved freight flow despite ongoing conflict.

events.title: Tariff fraud enforcement targets importers
events.description: Federal authorities are taking enforcement actions against importers, including First Brands, Perfectus, Canadian steel firms, and Greenbrier, for alleged duty evasion. This reflects increased scrutiny and potential disruptions in import compliance and trade flows.

events.title: Kuwait oil production recovery after Hormuz reopening
events.description: Kuwait Petroleum Company expects to restore 70% of its oil production within 6-8 weeks following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy chokepoint.

events.title: MSC vessel hit by projectiles at Um Qasr port
events.description: MSC's vessel MSC Sariska V was struck by two projectiles at Iraq's Um Qasr port amid rising Middle East tensions. The incident occurred during a stop at the port on Monday.

events.title: France releases detained tanker captain
events.description: French prosecutors confirmed the release of the captain of the tanker Tagor, which was detained by France on May 31 for suspected shadow fleet operations.

events.title: FedEx partner airline seeks FAA waiver for Caribbean routes
events.description: A FedEx air cargo contractor is at risk of losing Caribbean service due to FAA regulations limiting how far larger aircraft can fly over water. The airline is seeking a waiver to continue operations with bigger planes on these routes.

events.title: US imposes forced labour tariffs on EU and 59 countries
events.description: The US has rejected EU objections to new 10%-12.5% tariffs on imports from the EU and 59 other countries, citing failures to address forced labour in supply chains. This policy move could disrupt trade flows and increase costs for affected imports.

events.title: Mine-hunting drone deployed in Hormuz coalition
events.description: The Royal Navy has deployed a new mine-hunting drone in the Strait of Hormuz as part of a 15-nation coalition to clear naval mines and ensure safe passage for shipping.

events.title: Oil shipments increase through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has increased as traders use stealth measures to navigate the area. This reflects ongoing uncertainty in the energy market amid the Iran war.

events.title: Persian Gulf attacks disrupt oil flows
events.description: Fresh attacks in the Persian Gulf have raised concerns over the resumption of energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil chokepoint. The situation has pushed oil prices higher and created uncertainty in energy markets.
